questions.q: In Hindu tradition, Vamana is an avatar of which deity?
questions.explanation: Vamana is counted among the Dashavatara, the ten principal incarnations of Vishnu.

questions.q: In what physical form does Vishnu appear as the avatar Vamana?
questions.explanation: Vamana appears as a small dwarf brahmin, his name itself meaning 'dwarf'.

questions.q: How many steps of land does Vamana request from King Bali?
questions.explanation: Vamana asks only for the land he can cover in three steps.

questions.q: From which generous king does Vamana beg for land?
questions.explanation: Vamana approaches the generous asura king Bali to request three paces of land.

questions.q: What is the subtitle traditionally given to Vamana, the fifth avatar of Vishnu?
options: The Boar Who Lifted The Earth
questions.explanation: Vamana is known as 'The Dwarf Of Three Strides' for his three world-measuring steps.

questions.q: Into what towering cosmic form does Vamana grow to measure the worlds?
questions.explanation: Vamana grows into the cosmic Trivikrama, 'he of the three strides', to span the universe.

questions.q: To which realm is King Bali sent to rule after Vamana's third step?
questions.explanation: Vamana sends the honest Bali to rule the netherworld of Patala.

questions.q: In the classical list of Vishnu's ten avatars, Vamana is which number?
questions.explanation: Vamana is the fifth of the Dashavatara, following Matsya, Kurma, Varaha, and Narasimha.

questions.q: Which two realms does Vamana cover with his first two strides as Trivikrama?
questions.explanation: As Trivikrama, Vamana measures the earth with his first stride and the sky with his second.

questions.q: What does Vamana place his foot upon for his third and final stride?
questions.explanation: For the third stride, Vamana places his foot on Bali's head, pressing him to Patala.

questions.q: King Bali, whom Vamana humbles, was the grandson of which famous devotee of Vishnu?
questions.explanation: Bali was the grandson of Prahlada, the devoted son of the asura king Hiranyakashipu.

questions.q: Which Purana is the principal source for the detailed narrative of Vamana and King Bali?
questions.explanation: The Bhagavata Purana relates the full account of Vamana's request and Bali's generosity.

questions.q: What had King Bali achieved before Vamana approached him for land?
options: He had drunk the ocean dry
He had churned the nectar of immortality
He had conquered the three worlds
He had brought the Ganga to earth
questions.explanation: Bali had conquered the three worlds through his devotion and sacrifices before Vamana came.

questions.q: In which class of beings is the generous King Bali, humbled by Vamana, counted?
questions.explanation: Bali is an asura (Daitya) king, yet renowned for his righteousness and generosity.

questions.q: The Trivikrama form of Vamana is most directly associated with which earlier Vedic deity?
options: The Vishnu of the Rigveda's three strides
The Agni of the sacred fire
questions.explanation: The three world-measuring strides echo the Rigvedic Vishnu, praised for traversing the cosmos in three steps.

questions.q: What object does Vamana, as a brahmin, characteristically carry in his iconography?
questions.explanation: Vamana is typically depicted as a young brahmin carrying a parasol, staff, and water pot.

questions.q: Who tried to warn King Bali against granting Vamana's request for three paces of land?
questions.explanation: Bali's preceptor Shukracharya recognized Vamana as Vishnu and warned the king against the gift.

questions.q: On account of whose dominion over the three worlds does Vishnu descend as Vamana?
questions.explanation: Vishnu descends as Vamana because Bali's rule had displaced Indra and the devas from the heavens.

questions.q: How is the gift of land formally sealed when Bali agrees to Vamana's request?
options: By pouring water from a vessel
questions.explanation: Bali confirms the gift by the customary pouring of water, despite Shukracharya's attempt to block the spout.

questions.q: Which king of the devas is restored to his heavenly throne through Vamana's intervention?
questions.explanation: By reclaiming the three worlds from Bali, Vamana restores Indra to his rule over the heavens.

questions.q: Why does Vamana grant King Bali lordship of the netherworld rather than destroying him?
options: Because Bali defeated him in combat
Because of Bali's unbroken honesty and devotion
Because Bali fled before the third step
questions.explanation: Bali's unwavering honesty in honoring his promise earns him lordship of Patala rather than destruction.

questions.q: Vamana is described as descending as the son of which sage and his wife in the Puranas?
questions.explanation: The Puranas relate that Vamana was born to the sage Kashyapa and Aditi, mother of the gods.

questions.q: Because he is born to Aditi, Vamana is counted among which group of Vedic deities?
questions.explanation: As a son of Aditi, Vamana is reckoned among the Adityas, the children of Aditi.

questions.q: In the South Indian festival of Onam, the people commemorate the annual return of which figure linked to the Vamana story?
questions.explanation: Onam celebrates the yearly homecoming of the beloved King Mahabali, whom Vamana sent to Patala.

questions.q: According to the tradition, where did the water washing Trivikrama's foot, raised to the sky, become a sacred river?
questions.explanation: The Puranas relate that water from Brahma washing Trivikrama's uplifted foot became the celestial Ganga.

questions.q: Which avatar immediately precedes Vamana in the classical Dashavatara sequence?
questions.explanation: Narasimha, the fourth avatar, immediately precedes Vamana, the fifth, in the classical sequence.

questions.q: What boon does the humbled King Bali receive concerning a future cosmic age, according to the tradition?
options: He will become Indra in a future manvantara
He will be reborn as a Dashavatara
He will ascend to Vaikuntha at once
He will gain immortality on earth
questions.explanation: The tradition holds that Bali is destined to become the Indra of a future manvantara as a reward for his virtue.

questions.q: When Shukracharya tried to block the water-spout to stop Bali's gift to Vamana, what is said to have happened to him?
options: He lost an eye when the spout was pierced
questions.explanation: Vamana pierced the blocked spout with a blade of grass, costing Shukracharya one of his eyes.

questions.q: In the chronology of the four ages, Vamana's descent is traditionally placed in which yuga?
questions.explanation: Vamana's avatar is traditionally placed in the Satya Yuga, among the earliest of Vishnu's descents.

questions.q: Which central virtue does the Vamana episode most enduringly celebrate through the figure of King Bali?
options: Steadfast keeping of one's given word
questions.explanation: Bali's refusal to break his promise even when he recognizes Vishnu exalts the keeping of one's given word.
